code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test20-JUN-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely nephropathy, with possible tubular dysfunction. Low HbA1c excludes chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease.
